scope_boundary: Lightweight breadth validation only. This does not represent a full MSFT report audit.

verified_claims.report_excerpt: Microsoft continues to demonstrate robust financial performance, with FY24 revenue reaching $245.1 billion, up 15.7% year-over-year.
verified_claims.extraction_note: Historical revenue value separated from the same sentence's year-over-year growth calculation.

verification_source.document_title: Microsoft Corporation Form 10-K for fiscal year ended June 30, 2024

verified_claims.evidence_excerpt: Consolidated statements of income report revenue of $245,122 million for fiscal 2024.
verified_claims.calculation_trace: 245,122 million = $245.122B, which rounds to $245.1B at one decimal place.
verified_claims.review_note: Strictly supported by Microsoft FY2024 10-K. Report-level traceability remains weak because the FinRobot report does not identify the specific filing or line item.

verified_claims.report_excerpt: Microsoft continues to demonstrate robust financial performance, with FY24 revenue reaching $245.1 billion, up 15.7% year-over-year.
verified_claims.normalized_claim: Microsoft fiscal 2024 revenue increased by approximately 15.7% year over year.
verified_claims.extraction_note: Derived calculation separated from the reported FY2024 revenue value.

verification_source.document_title: Microsoft Corporation Form 10-K for fiscal year ended June 30, 2024

verified_claims.evidence_excerpt: Consolidated statements of income report revenue of $245,122 million for fiscal 2024 and $211,915 million for fiscal 2023. Management discussion reports total revenue increased by $33.2 billion or 16%.
verified_claims.calculation_trace: (245,122 - 211,915) / 211,915 = 0.15670 = 15.7% when rounded to one decimal place.
verified_claims.review_note: Strictly supported by exact source values and reproducible calculation. Report-level traceability remains weak.

verified_claims.report_excerpt: Relative to peers, Microsoft’s EBITDA outpaces Amazon, though Amazon is expected to narrow the gap by 2026. Microsoft’s EV/EBITDA valuation remains at a premium (23.6x for 2025E vs. Amazon’s 15.3x), reflecting its stronger margins, stable growth, and diversified business mix.
verified_claims.claim_quote: EV/EBITDA valuation remains at a premium (23.6x for 2025E vs. Amazon’s 15.3x)
verified_claims.normalized_claim: The report states that MSFT 2025E EV/EBITDA is 23.6x versus AMZN 15.3x.
verified_claims.extraction_note: Forward valuation multiple separated from the broader peer-comparison interpretation.

verification_source.document_title: No dated market or consensus source mapped in this lightweight validation

verified_claims.evidence_excerpt: No dated enterprise value, net debt, EBITDA estimate, or peer metric source has been mapped for this claim.
verified_claims.review_note: Forward EV/EBITDA requires dated market value, net debt or EV basis, EBITDA estimate basis, and peer-source provenance before deterministic scoring. This claim is recorded for breadth validation but excluded from the L2 denominator.

verified_claims.report_excerpt: Key growth drivers include Azure’s continued leadership in cloud infrastructure, expansion in AI-powered productivity tools (such as Copilot), and resilient performance in Office 365 and LinkedIn.
verified_claims.claim_quote: Azure’s continued leadership in cloud infrastructure, expansion in AI-powered productivity tools (such as Copilot)
verified_claims.normalized_claim: The report identifies Azure leadership and AI-powered productivity tools such as Copilot as key growth drivers.
verified_claims.extraction_note: Narrative AI/cloud growth-driver claim separated from numeric revenue and margin claims.

verification_source.document_title: Microsoft Corporation Form 10-K for fiscal year ended June 30, 2024

verified_claims.evidence_excerpt: Microsoft Cloud revenue increased 23% to $137.4 billion. Azure and other cloud services revenue increased 29%, including 8 points from AI services.
verified_claims.review_note: Primary-source context supports cloud and AI growth relevance, but the report's leadership and growth-driver framing is an analytical inference. It is not included in deterministic scoring.
